articles.title: Chennai Weather LATEST Update: Heavy Rain Alert Across Tamil Nadu Until May 31; IMD Predicts Cooler Weather
articles.description: Chennai Weather LATEST Update: Tamil Nadu is likely to witness heavy rain, thunderstorms, strong winds till May 31 due to upper air cyclonic circulation systems over nearby regions. Showers are expected to bring relief from the intense summer heat

articles.title: Michelle Obama reveals husband Barack Obama is a 'bully' for Steven Spielberg's movies' early screenings; filmmaker recalls his visit to set: "It was a religious experience"
articles.description: Steven Spielberg shared a memorable story about Barack Obama secretly visiting the set of his upcoming sci-fi thriller Disclosure Day during an appearance on Michelle Obama’s podcast.

articles.title: Several feared dead as portion of under-construction bridge collapses in Uttar Pradesh
articles.description: The portion of an under-construction bridge over Betwa river in Uttar Pradesh collapsed during a storm on Friday, with at least six feared dead and several injured.

articles.title: “I was a stalker fan”: Shahana Goswami on dating Milind Soman despite 21-year age gap and moving on quickly after breakup
articles.description: Actress Shahana Goswami candidly shared details about her past relationship with Milind Soman. She revealed their unexpected connection began with her being a teenage fan, leading to a six-year texting exchange before they dated. Despite their love and a 21-year age gap, they parted ways in 2013 due to differing life aspirations and a desire for freedom. She added that she found clarity soon after the breakup, and formula of relationships doesnt suit her.

articles.title: 4 Lakh Students, 11 Lakh Answer Scripts, One Big OSM Row: CBSE Re-Evaluation Window Opens Today
articles.description: CBSE Class 12 re-evaluation window is set to open today amid an escalating controversy over the board’s newly introduced On-Screen Marking (OSM) system.

articles.title: Petrol And Diesel Prices Today: Check Latest Fuel Rates In Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai And More
articles.description: Fuel prices in India are stable but remain elevated, with significant variations between states due to different local taxes like VAT. Petrol and diesel rates in major cities continue to be high. Prices are determined daily by international crude oil rates, currency fluctuations, and government levies.

articles.title: Knicks star Mitchell Robinson suffers fractured finger ahead of NBA Finals
articles.description: New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson has sustained a fractured right pinkie finger. With the 2026 NBA Finals approaching, find the latest on his injury status and recovery timeline.

articles.title: Fever vs. Valkyries: Injury update, start time, how to watch and more
articles.description: The Indiana Fever and Golden State Valkyries meet Thursday night at Chase Center in San Francisco for their second matchup in less than a week. Indiana enters riding a three-game winning streak behind Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston, and the league’s highest-scoring offense. Golden State responds with balanced scoring and one of the WNBA’s strongest defensive units.

articles.title: Equity mutual funds deliver up to 17% return in May led by international funds. Should you go global?
articles.description: Equity mutual funds saw strong performance in May, with returns reaching up to 17%. International funds led the gains, driven by markets like the US, Korea, and Taiwan. While global diversification is beneficial, experts advise caution due to high valuations. Investors should consider a systematic investment plan for global exposure, focusing on long-term data for market assessment.

articles.title: Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo: Mongbwalu residents split over disease threat and government response
articles.description: An Ebola outbreak is devastating Mongbwalu in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. Many residents deny the virus's existence, complicating response efforts. Healthcare workers face challenges with limited resources. The outbreak has spread to nearby provinces and Uganda. The World Health Organization declared it an international emergency. Authorities are working to contain the spread.

articles.title: Jannik Sinner’s shocking French Open defeat makes girlfriend Laila Hasanovic’s recent video impossible to ignore now
articles.description: Jannik Sinner’s shocking second-round defeat to Juan Manuel Cerundolo at the 2026 French Open has shaken the tournament and reopened concerns about the world No. 1’s struggles in extreme heat. Despite organizers seemingly trying to help him with an earlier match slot, Sinner cramped during the contest and failed to handle the conditions. The upset has also drawn attention to girlfriend Laila Hasanovic’s recent viral TikTok clip, which fans are now viewing differently after the loss. With Sinner out and Carlos Alcaraz injured, the French Open men’s draw suddenly feels wide open.

articles.title: Why Is the Trump Administration Investigating LinkedIn Co-Founder Reid Hoffman's Nonprofit?
articles.description: The Department of Justice is investigating Reid Hoffman's nonprofit, American Future Republic, due to its financial contributions that supported E. Jean Carroll's legal case against Trump. Hoffman has long been a major financial backer of the Democratic Party and its causes, donating millions over the years.

articles.title: NCB places habitual narcotics offender in prevention detention to curb drug-trafficking
articles.description: Referred to informally as a “scientist” by his peers in a nod to his technical prowess, Rahul Shedge, a post-graduate in organic chemistry, is allegedly adept at setting up facilities for the manufacturing of illicit drugs and allied chemicals.

articles.title: After early miscarriage, some parents turn to cremation for closure: 'It made the pregnancy loss feel real'
articles.description: When miscarriage happens in the first trimester and there may be little to see or recover, some parents turn to cremation to grieve a loss that can otherwise feel invisible.

articles.title: Rory McIlroy and Erica Stoll give fans unexpected relationship glimpse during glamorous White House night together
articles.description: Rory McIlroy and Erica Stoll made a rare public appearance together at the White House State Dinner hosted by Donald Trump. The couple looked happy in a newly shared photo after reconciling last year. Trump also praised McIlroy’s recent Masters victory during his speech, making the golfer one of the standout guests at the glamorous Washington event.

articles.title: HC penalises man for harassing elderly mother through persistent litigation
articles.description: The Bombay High Court upheld a ruling favoring a mother over her son, imposing ₹50,000 in costs for his harassment during her twilight years.

articles.title: Knicks’ Mitchell Robinson Breaks pinky Finger: When Will He Return, and How Did It Happen? Here’s what we know
articles.description: New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson has a right pinky finger injury. There is no timeline for his return. This comes as the Knicks prepare for their first NBA Finals since 1999. Robinson is a key player for the team. His absence will be felt in the championship series. The Knicks face the Western Conference champion next week.

articles.title: Youth dies after falling into pit at illegal sand mining site
articles.description: Wardha: A 35-year-old man died after falling into a deep pit allegedly created due to illegal sand excavation at Shekapur (Bai) sand ghat in Hingangha.

articles.title: Cyprus’s desire to buy BrahMos, Kamikaze drones from India alarms Turkey
articles.description: Cyprus is seeking to acquire BrahMos cruise missiles and kamikaze drones from India, a move that has alarmed Türkiye. This potential defense deal, possibly part of an EU program, could significantly alter the regional military balance in the Eastern Mediterranean. The acquisition follows a roadmap for bilateral defense cooperation between India and Cyprus.

articles.description: Families are encouraged to consume fortified rice to enhance nutrient intake and improve overall health, especially in Karnataka.

articles.description: Chandrapur: A three-year-old tigress died after being struck by a railway engine on the Gondia–Ballarshah railway line in Chandrapur district on Thurs.

articles.description: On Wednesday afternoon, her anxious parents learned that Vidhi was stabbed to death during an attempted robbery in Canada’s Niagara region on May 15.

articles.title: Israel Faces Growing Threat From Hezbollah’s Fibre-Optic Kamikaze Drones
articles.description: Hezbollah's increasing deployment of fibre-optic drones along the Israel-Lebanon border is significantly altering the conflict dynamics, posing severe threats to Israeli military and civilian populations.

articles.title: Brothers get life-saving heart transplants 11 years apart in Delhi
articles.description: Two brothers underwent successful life-saving heart transplants 11 years apart after both were diagnosed with advanced Dilated Cardiomyopathy, a progressive heart condition. The rare case highlights a shared genetic risk in the family.

articles.title: ‘Just left my second job this year due to having a boss who screams and throws things, is this the new norm?’: This viral story exposed what millions of American workers are too afraid to say
articles.description: The article discusses a viral story about a woman who quit two jobs due to abusive bosses, highlighting the alarming prevalence of workplace bullying in America and the effects it has on employees.

articles.description: The departure of Karnataka's tall leaders signals a pivotal shift in the state's political landscape and future dynamics.

articles.title: Centre, Delhi govt launch major road projects for future-ready NCR
articles.description: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta announced a series of large-scale road projects jointly undertaken by the Centre and Delhi Government. The projects aim to ease traffic congestion and modernise the transport network in the Delhi-NCR region with new expressways.

articles.title: ‘US deserves mentally fit Prez’: ‘SLEEPY’ Trump faces backlash over dozing off during cabinet meet
articles.description: Donald Trump is facing massive backlash online after viral clips appeared to show him dozing off during a cabinet meeting, Memorial Day event, and White House appearances. Social media users revived the “Sleepy Joe” nickname once used against Joe Biden, with critics now mocking Trump as “Sleepy Donald” and “Slump Trump” amid growing scrutiny over his health and fitness. The controversy exploded after Trump seemingly nodded off while Marco Rubio discussed Iran, the Strait of Hormuz, and U.S. foreign policy during a high-stakes cabinet meeting.

articles.title: Jannik Sinner crashes out of Roland Garros, 30-match winning streak ends at hands of Juan Manuel Cerundolo
articles.description: An ailing Jannik Sinner fell in five sets to Argentinian Juan Manuel Cerundolo; the men's number one seed knocked out despite serving for the match.

articles.title: 7 Protective Dog Breeds That Are Ideal For Families With Kids
articles.description: Finding a protective dog requires balancing a natural guardian instinct with a gentle, patient temperament suitable for life with children.

articles.title: PM Modi congratulates new Slovenian PM Janez Jansa, eyes stronger ties
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated Janez Jansa on his election as Slovenia's new PM, looking forward to strengthening bilateral ties. Slovenia's parliament voted to bring back the right-wing leader, ending a post-election deadlock.

articles.title: VPL: Defending title brings unnecessary load, says Jitesh Sharma
articles.description: 'Defending the title gives extra unnecessary load,' says NECO Master Blaster captain Jitesh Sharma ahead of VPL. He contrasts the mindset of defending with chasing, urging his players to be fearless and focus on improving as cricketers.

articles.title: Gujarat: Modern nursery farming boosts chilli yields for Rajkot farmers
articles.description: Farmers in Gujarat's Rajkot are shifting to scientific nursery farming for chilli cultivation. This modern approach, supported by government subsidies for nurseries and irrigation, is boosting crop yields, reducing losses, and improving farmer incomes.

articles.title: Keanu Reeves supports 'White Horse' director Carl Rinsch before sentencing: 'In my opinion, Carl is an exceptional artist'
articles.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves has appealed to a federal judge for leniency for filmmaker Carl Rinsch, convicted in an $11 million Netflix fraud case. Rinsch, who directed Reeves in '47 Ronin', faces sentencing for allegedly misusing funds meant for the unfinished sci-fi series 'White Horse'. Reeves described Rinsch as a visionary artist and a friend.

articles.title: Iran backs Oman after Trump threatens to ‘blow them up’ over Hormuz dispute: Why Muskat finds itself at the centre of crisis
articles.description: The latest confrontation unfolded as fragile negotiations between Tehran and Washington over reopening the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be stalling once again.

articles.title: Park Medi shares to remain in focus as Brokerage sees 22% upside
articles.description: The stock has gained 91 percent on a year-to-date basis, according to data available on the exchanges.

articles.title: Nithin Kamath is worried about Indian farmers; Zerodha CEO explains how Strait of Hormuz closure could seriously hurt everyone
articles.description: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath has warned about India’s heavy reliance on imported fertilisers and raw materials used in fertiliser production, calling it a major vulnerability for the country’s agriculture sector. Amid global supply chain disruptions and rising energy prices, Kamath stressed the urgent need to reduce this dependency. He also highlighted the importance of innovation, sustainable farming practices and investment in alternatives such as bio-inputs, organic manure, green hydrogen and biogas to help India move towards greater agricultural self-sufficiency.

articles.title: Dharmendra Pradhan chairs review meeting on CBSE Class 12 evaluation, post-result process
articles.description: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan met with CBSE officials to review Class 12 evaluation and post-result processes. The meeting addressed concerns regarding the On Screen Marking System. Discussions focused on improving the re-evaluation portal and digital platforms. Plans were outlined for enhanced student support and transparent service delivery. Measures for student benefit were highlighted.

articles.title: Former BP chair Albert Manifold denies wrongdoing after ouster
articles.description: Albert Manifold, the former BP Chair, has denied misconduct following his dismissal. He acknowledged pushing for changes in costs and efficiency. Manifold stated that he does not accept lies being told about him. He also described the current BP leadership as having integrity. Manifold has reportedly hired a law firm after his removal from the position.

articles.title: NHAI finalises 1,692.5km highway list for ₹30,000 cr FY27 monetisation push
articles.description: These 17 projects identified for FY26-27 exclude assets proposed for monetisation through the newly self-sponsored Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust (RIIT), which will be a separate monetisation vehicle

articles.title: Beyond combat capability, how AMCA will help India beat China in the long race
articles.description: India's Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program is a catalyst for national industrial growth, extending beyond defense. It fosters advancements in materials science, AI, and manufacturing, creating vital spillovers for civilian sectors like automotive and renewable energy. This initiative strengthens India's technological ecosystem and workforce, positioning it as a global aerospace supplier.

articles.title: Ahead of new government, Siddaramaiah's policy man Venkateshaiah exits CMO after transfer
articles.description: KAS officer Venkateshaiah, a long-time collaborator with outgoing Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, was transferred on Thursday ahead of a new government stepping in.
